A WEAK EF0 TORNADO TRAVELED EAST THROUGH HILLSBORO, MO JUST NORTH OF MAIN STREET. TREE DAMAGE AND MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS NOTED ALONG THE 0.57 MILE PATH WITH A MAXIMUM WIND SPEED OF 77 MPH AND A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 198 YARDS.

A WEAK EF1 TORNADO FORMED AT 630 PM JUST NORTHEAST OF FESTUS ALONG OLD A HWY. IT MOVED NORTHEASTWARD WITH MINOR DAMAGE TO TREES AND STRUCTURES BEFORE LIFTING AND DISSIPATING IN DUNKIN-FLETCHER MEMORIAL PARK AT 635 PM. THE TORNADO HAD MAX WINDS OF 93 MPH AND A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 360 YARDS. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 3.97 MILES.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 2 WNW FESTUS. A WEAK EF1 TORNADO FORMED AT 630 PM JUST NORTHEAST OF FESTUS ALONG OLD A HWY. IT MOVED NORTHEASTWARD WITH MINOR DAMAGE TO TREES AND STRUCTURES BEFORE LIFTING AND DISSIPATING IN DUNKIN-FLETCHER MEMORIAL PARK AT 635 PM. THE TORNADO HAD MAX WINDS OF 100 MPH AND A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 360 YARDS. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 3.97 MILES.

A WEAK TORNADO TRAVELED 16.3 MILES ACROSS MONROE COUNTY, ILLINOIS BETWEEN 6:34 PM AND 6:56 PM. THE TORNADO BEGAN NEAR THE MISSISSIPPI RIVER SOUTHWEST OF MAEYSTOWN AND TRAVERSED RURAL AREAS CAUSING SPORADIC TREE AND STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO HECKER, IL. THE MAXIMUM WIND SPEED OF 100 MPH CORRESPONDED TO THE DAMAGE JUST NORTH OF HECKER WHERE OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED.

A WEAK EF1 TORNADO FORMED JUST NORTHWEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF I-55 AND HIGHWAY Z. IT TRAVELED SOUTHEAST TO COMMERCIAL BLVD. MINOR DAMAGE TO TREES AND STRUCTURES WERE NOTED. THE TORNADO HAD MAX WINDS OF 100 MPH AND A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 150 YARDS. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR LESS THAN A MILE.
